source.notes: Discovered via pitstop-check@0.1.1. The retry primitive in src/infra/retry.ts supports retryAfterMs correctly. Callers in src/agents/venice-models.ts and others do not pass retryAfterMs. When Venice API returns Retry-After: 600, client ignores it and retries on own schedule causing 10-minute timeout block.

hazard.summary: Correct retry primitive, unwired call sites — retryAfterMs supported but never passed by callers, causing Retry-After headers to be silently ignored under concurrency
signals: src/agents/venice-models.ts:24 — All 429s treated as retryable — CAP vs WAIT not distinguished
Retry-After: 600 returned by Venice API — client retries immediately on own schedule
pitstop-check flagged 22 issues across src/agents directory

constraints: retryAfterMs must be passed at every call site that handles 429 responses
Retry-After header value must be used as the floor for retry delay
CAP (concurrency/quota limit) and WAIT (transient burst) must be distinguished at call site level
knobs: retryAfterMs — callback that reads Retry-After header from error response
maxElapsedMs — hard cap on total retry window
shouldRetry — per-call-site classification of retryable vs terminal errors
verification: Run pitstop-check against src/agents — should return zero warnings after wiring retryAfterMs at all call sites
Trigger Venice API rate limit — verify client waits exactly Retry-After duration before retrying
Confirm issue #49811 timeout block does not recur after fix
